human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for architecture-and-engineering deliverable specificity, source quality, revision and transmittal control, seal and responsible-control boundaries, validated documentation, and scenario completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to architecture-and-engineering client deliverable handling tied to deliverable registers, transmittals, revision history, BIM and CDE records, handover artifacts, and disciplined escalation.
Authority sources cover public-owner design submission and record-package guidance, recognized BIM and information-exchange standards, and professional seal and responsible-control boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported exceptions, repeat delivery failures, uncontrolled issue paths, in-scope turnover handling, authority gaps, source conflicts, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/architecture-and-engineering-services/client-deliverables-specialist/ so client deliverable handling stays separate from design authority, permitting, contracts, QA or QC, and commissioning ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around seal status, release control, design interpretation, contractual requests, and uncontrolled document issue paths.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated client deliverables specialist that supports evidence-backed stakeholder communication, revision control, and policy-aware escalation in architecture-and-engineering services.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
